title: Design System: Portfolio Dashboard — Indian Markets

display.purpose: The centered hero portfolio value, in gold. Once per overview.

brand.purpose: The PORTFOLIO sidebar logotype — the only wide-tracked element.

body.purpose: Nav labels, controls, prose (cap prose at 65–75ch).

label.purpose: Small tracked uppercase that names a figure without competing with it.

motion.name: control-ease
motion.purpose: Button/nav state transitions — the control lights to the lamp on contact, then releases.

motion.name: shimmer
motion.purpose: Skeleton-loader gradient sweep while the cold data load runs. Hold steady under prefers-reduced-motion.

motion.name: click-spark
motion.purpose: Gold sparks radiating from every click. Respect prefers-reduced-motion.

components.name: Hero Value
components.description: The single loudest moment in the product — centered portfolio value in gold with a sign-carrying P&L line. Used once per overview.
components.html:
Total Portfolio Value
₹48,21,650
+₹3,14,200 P&L (+6.97%)
across 4 accounts · 37 holdings
components.css: .ds-hero { text-align: center; padding: 1.4rem 0 0.6rem; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-hero-label { text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.16em; font-size: 0.8rem; color: #8B8B8B; } .ds-hero-value { font-size: 3.4rem; font-weight: 800; color: #C9A87A; line-height: 1.05; margin: 0.2rem 0; letter-spacing: -0.02em; } .ds-hero-pnl { font-size: 1.15rem; font-weight: 600; } .ds-gain { color: #3DDC97; } .ds-loss { color: #F0564A; } .ds-hero-sub { color: #8B8B8B; font-size: 0.9rem; margin-top: 0.2rem; }

components.name: Metric Card
components.description: Per-figure card: small uppercase muted label, large ink value, mint/coral delta. The unit of the scannable dashboard.
components.html:
Day's Change
₹12,480
▲ +0.41%
components.css: .ds-metric { background: #141414; border: 1px solid #262626; border-radius: 14px; padding: 1rem 1.1rem; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; min-width: 180px; } .ds-metric-label { text-transform: uppercase; letter-spacing: 0.08em; font-size: 0.72rem; font-weight: 600; color: #8B8B8B; } .ds-metric-value { font-size: 1.65rem; font-weight: 700; color: #EDEDED; margin-top: 0.25rem; } .ds-metric-delta { font-size: 0.9rem; font-weight: 600; margin-top: 0.1rem; } .ds-gain { color: #3DDC97; } .ds-loss { color: #F0564A; }

components.name: Container Card
components.description: The larger wrapper around charts and tables. Flat — depth from tonal step + hairline border, never shadow. Never nested.
components.html:
Allocation by Sector
chart / table region
components.css: .ds-container { background: #121212; border: 1px solid #232323; border-radius: 16px; padding: 1.1rem; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-container-title { font-size: 1.5rem; font-weight: 700; color: #EDEDED; letter-spacing: -0.01em; margin-bottom: 0.5rem; } .ds-container-body { color: #8B8B8B; font-size: 0.95rem; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.description: The only button style — understated. Lights to gold on hover/focus. There is no filled primary; gold is spent on data, not controls.
components.css: .ds-btn { font-family: Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; font-weight: 500; font-size: 0.92rem; color: #EDEDED; background: #161616; border: 1px solid #2A2A2A; border-radius: 10px; padding: 0.45rem 0.95rem; cursor: pointer; transition: border-color 150ms ease, color 150ms ease; } .ds-btn:hover { border-color: #C9A87A; color: #C9A87A; } .ds-btn:focus-visible { outline: none; border-color: #C9A87A; color: #C9A87A; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(201,168,122,0.35); } .ds-btn:active { background: #1a1a1a; }

components.name: Nav Item
components.description: Sidebar nav (a restyled radio group). Selected item is the only lit row — gold label on a gold-tinted black.
components.html:
📊 Overview
📋 Holdings
📈 Performance
components.css: .ds-nav { background: #0C0C0C; padding: 0.4rem; border-radius: 12px; display: flex; flex-direction: column; gap: 2px; font-family: Inter, -apple-system, sans-serif; } .ds-nav-item { display: flex; align-items: center; padding: 0.5rem 0.7rem; border-radius: 10px; font-size: 0.95rem; font-weight: 500; color: #B9B9B9; cursor: pointer; transition: background 150ms ease; } .ds-nav-item:hover { background: #161616; } .ds-nav-selected { background: #18170F; color: #C9A87A; font-weight: 600; } .ds-nav-selected:hover { background: #18170F; }

components.name: Skeleton Loader
components.description: Dashboard-shaped loading state — never a spinner. Shimmer bars mirror the real page geometry. Hold steady under reduced motion.
components.css: .ds-sk { background: linear-gradient(90deg, #141414 25%, #1F1F1F 37%, #141414 63%); background-size: 400% 100%; animation: ds-sksh 1.4s ease infinite; border-radius: 8px; } @keyframes ds-sksh { 0% { background-position: 100% 0; } 100% { background-position: 0 0; } } .ds-sk-card { background: #121212; border: 1px solid #232323; border-radius: 14px; padding: 1.1rem; } @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .ds-sk { animation: none; background: #1a1a1a; } }

narrative.overview: The analyst's desk after market close: the room is dark, the screen is the only light source, and a single warm lamp — champagne gold — pools over the figures that matter. Everything is built so the numbers glow and the chrome recedes. A near-black canvas, surfaces stacked in tight tonal steps, one accent held to a tenth of any screen, and mint/coral reserved strictly for profit and loss. Nothing blinks, nothing celebrates, nothing hurries; the cadence is reflective. Restrained in the strict sense: tinted near-blacks plus one accent. Density is welcome where the data earns it, never manufactured by decoration. Depth is conveyed by tone, not shadow — one surface sits above another because it is a half-step lighter, the way objects separate under low light.
keyCharacteristics: Low-light dark room; data is the only thing that glows.
One accent (champagne gold), held rare; restraint is the identity.
Tonal layering over shadows — depth from lightness steps, surfaces flat at rest.
Inter everywhere, in weight — no display/body pairing.
Numbers are the hero: typography and spacing do the work, decoration does none.
P&L never relies on color alone — sign and figure carry the meaning too.

rules.name: The One Lamp Rule
rules.body: Champagne gold appears on no more than ~10% of any screen, and only to mark the brand, the single headline figure, the current selection, or a state. The moment gold is used decoratively, the room stops being lit and starts being painted.

rules.name: The P&L Is Not Just Color Rule
rules.body: Gains and losses are never communicated by mint vs. coral alone. The sign, the formatted figure, or a label always rides alongside the color, so a color-blind user reads the position correctly.

rules.name: The One Family Rule
rules.body: Inter does everything. Introducing a second typeface reads as costume, not craft. Range lives in weight and size, never in family.

rules.name: The Quiet Caps Rule
rules.body: Uppercase is reserved for small tracked labels and the brand wordmark. Never set a heading or a value in caps.

rules.name: The Tonal-Depth Rule
rules.body: Separation between surfaces is achieved with the panel-step ramp plus a hairline border, never with box-shadow. A drop shadow on a card here is a bug, not a style.

dos: Do keep champagne gold (#C9A87A) to ~10% of any screen — brand, the one headline figure, current selection, and states only.
Do convey gains/losses with sign and figure plus color, never mint/coral alone — AA color-blind safety is non-negotiable in a finance tool.
Do create depth with the panel-step ramp (#0C0C0C→#161616) and hairline borders, not shadows.
Do set everything in Inter, varying weight (400–800) and size for hierarchy.

Do verify text contrast against the near-black canvas: #EDEDED/#B9B9B9 are safe; #8B8B8B (5.8:1) and #808080 (5.0:1) pass AA — never drop text below #808080 (#555555 and darker are disabled glyphs and hairlines only).
Do show the dashboard-shaped skeleton on cold loads; never a centered spinner.
Do let tables and panels run dense when the data earns it; lead with the consolidated picture and keep heavy analysis behind toggles.
donts: Don't build the loud retail trading app — no neon green/red, no gamified gains, no confetti.
Don't build the cluttered Bloomberg terminal — never dump wall-to-wall data with no hierarchy or air.
Don't drift toward cartoonish/playful fintech — no mascots, bright illustration, or 'friendly money' styling.
Don't ship generic SaaS slop: no purple gradients, no gradient text, no identical icon-card grids, no hero-metric template.
Don't put a box-shadow under a card, or use a border-left/border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe.
Don't introduce a second font family, or set headings/values in uppercase.
Don't spend gold on decoration — the moment it stops marking meaning, the lamp metaphor dies.
Don't nest a card inside a card.
